flags.description: questions JSON array, max 10 items. Each item requires "title"(field title) and "type"(text/number/select/datetime/user/attachment/location). Optional fields: "description"(plain text or markdown link like [text](https://example.com)),"required","option_display_mode"(0=dropdown/1=vertical/2=horizontal,select only),"multiple"(bool,select/user),"options"([{"name":"opt","hue":"Blue"}],select only),"style"({"type":"plain/phone/url/email/barcode/rating","precision":2,"format":"yyyy/MM/dd","icon":"star","min":1,"max":5}). description: Create a Lark document

flags.name: wiki-space
flags.description: wiki space ID (use my_library for personal library)

description: Fetch Lark document content
Download document media or whiteboard thumbnail (auto-detects extension)
Insert a local image or file into a Lark document (4-step orchestration + auto-rollback); appends to end by default, or inserts relative to a text selection with --selection-with-ellipsis

flags.name: before
flags.description: before the matched block instead of after (requires --selection-with-ellipsis)

flags.name: file
flags.description: local file path (files > 20MB use multipart upload automatically)

flags.name: file-view
flags.description: file block rendering: card (default) | preview | inline; only applies when --type=file. preview renders audio/video as an inline player

flags.name: from-clipboard
flags.description: image from system clipboard instead of a local file (macOS/Windows built-in; Linux requires xclip, xsel or wl-paste)

flags.name: height
flags.description: image display height in pixels (only for --type=image); if --width is omitted it is auto-computed from the source image aspect ratio

flags.name: selection-with-ellipsis
flags.description: plain text (or 'start...end' to disambiguate) matching the target block's content. Media is inserted at the top-level ancestor of the matched block — i.e., when the selection is inside a callout, table cell, or nested list, media lands outside that container, not inside it. Pass 'start...end' (a unique prefix and suffix separated by '...') when the plain text appears in more than one block

flags.name: width
flags.description: image display width in pixels (only for --type=image); if --height is omitted it is auto-computed from the source image aspect ratio

description: Preview document media file (auto-detects extension)
Upload media file (image/attachment) to a document block

flags.name: file
flags.description: local file path (files > 20MB use multipart upload automatically)

flags.name: parent-node
flags.description: parent node ID (block_id for docx, board_token for whiteboard)

description: Search Lark docs, Wiki, and spreadsheet files (Search v2: doc_wiki/search)
Update a Lark document

flags.name: markdown
flags.description: new content (Lark-flavored Markdown; create blank whiteboards with <whiteboard type="blank"></whiteboard>, repeat to create multiple boards) (supports @file, - for stdin)

flags.name: mode
flags.description: update mode: append | overwrite | replace_range | replace_all | insert_before | insert_after | delete_range

description: Update an existing whiteboard in lark document with mermaid, plantuml or whiteboard dsl. refer to lark-whiteboard skill for more details.

flags.name: idempotent-token
flags.description: idempotent token to ensure the update is idempotent. Default is empty. min length is 10.

flags.name: input_format
flags.description: format of input data: raw | plantuml | mermaid. Default is raw.

flags.name: overwrite
flags.description: the whiteboard content, delete all existing content before update. Default is false.

flags.name: whiteboard-token
flags.description: whiteboard token of the whiteboard to update. You will need edit permission to update the whiteboard.

flags.name: doc
flags.description: document URL/token, sheet/slides URL, or wiki URL that resolves to doc/docx/sheet/slides

flags.name: full-comment
flags.description: a full-document comment; also the default when no location is provided

flags.name: type
flags.description: document type: doc, docx, sheet, slides (required when --doc is a bare token; auto-detected for URLs) (doc|docx|sheet|slides)

flags.name: remark
flags.description: optional note shown on the request card sent to the owner

flags.name: type
flags.description: target type; auto-inferred from URL when omitted (doc|sheet|file|wiki|bitable|docx|mindnote|slides)

flags.name: type
flags.description: file type (file, docx, bitable, doc, sheet, mindnote, folder, shortcut, slides)

description: Download a file from Drive to local
Export a doc/docx/sheet/bitable to a local file with limited polling

flags.name: doc-type
flags.description: source document type: doc | docx | sheet | bitable (doc|docx|sheet|bitable)

flags.name: file-extension
flags.description: export format: docx | pdf | xlsx | csv | markdown | base (bitable only) (docx|pdf|xlsx|csv|markdown|base)

flags.name: sub-id
flags.description: sub-table/sheet ID, required when exporting sheet/bitable as csv

description: Download an exported file by file_token
Import a local file to Drive as a cloud document (docx, sheet, bitable)

flags.name: file
flags.description: local file path (e.g. .docx, .xlsx, .md, .base; large files auto use multipart upload; .base is capped at 20MB)

flags.name: folder-token
flags.description: target folder token (omit for root folder; API accepts empty mount_key as root)

flags.name: name
flags.description: imported file name (default: local file name without extension)

description: Move a file or folder to another location in Drive

flags.name: file-token
flags.description: file or folder token to move

flags.name: type
flags.description: file type (file, docx, bitable, doc, sheet, mindnote, folder, slides)

description: One-way file-level mirror of a Drive folder onto a local directory (Drive → local)

flags.name: delete-local
flags.description: local regular files absent from Drive (file-level mirror; empty directories are NOT pruned); requires --yes

flags.name: if-exists
flags.description: policy when a local file already exists (skip = never touch existing files; smart = skip when local mtime is already up to date; overwrite = always replace) (overwrite|smart|skip) (default "overwrite")

flags.name: on-duplicate-remote
flags.description: policy when multiple remote Drive entries map to the same rel_path (fail|rename|newest|oldest) (default "fail")

description: File-level mirror of a local directory onto a Drive folder (local → Drive; remote-only directories are not removed)

flags.name: delete-remote
flags.description: Drive files absent locally (file-level mirror; remote-only directories are not removed); requires --yes

flags.name: if-exists
flags.description: policy when a Drive file already exists at the same rel_path (skip = never touch existing remote files; smart = skip when remote modified_time already matches or is newer, otherwise fall through to overwrite semantics; overwrite = always replace) (overwrite|smart|skip) (default "skip")

flags.name: on-duplicate-remote
flags.description: policy when multiple remote Drive entries map to the same rel_path (fail|newest|oldest) (default "fail")

description: Search Lark docs, Wiki, and spreadsheet files with flat filters (Search v2: doc_wiki/search)

flags.name: creator-ids
flags.description: comma-separated creator open_ids; mutually exclusive with --mine

flags.name: edited-since
flags.description: start of [my edited] time window (e.g. 7d, 1m, 1y, 2026-04-01, RFC3339, unix seconds)

flags.name: folder-tokens
flags.description: comma-separated folder tokens (doc-only; mutually exclusive with --space-ids)

flags.name: mine
flags.description: to docs I created (uses current user's open_id)

flags.name: page-token
flags.description: pagination token from a previous response

flags.name: query
flags.description: search keyword (may be empty to browse by filter only)

flags.name: space-ids
flags.description: comma-separated wiki space IDs (wiki-only; mutually exclusive with --folder-tokens)

description: Compare a local directory with a Drive folder by exact hash or quick modified_time

flags.name: quick
flags.description: modified_time only and skip remote downloads for files present on both sides

description: Two-way sync between a local directory and a Drive folder

flags.name: on-conflict
flags.description: conflict resolution when both sides modified a file (local-wins|remote-wins|keep-both|ask) (default "remote-wins")

flags.name: on-duplicate-remote
flags.description: policy when multiple remote Drive entries map to the same rel_path (fail|newest|oldest) (default "fail")

flags.name: quick
flags.description: best-effort modified_time comparison instead of SHA-256 hash; mismatched timestamps can still trigger real sync writes

description: Poll async task result for import, export, drive move/delete, wiki move, or wiki delete-space operations

flags.name: file-token
flags.description: source document token used for export task status lookup

flags.name: scenario
flags.description: task scenario: import, export, task_check, wiki_move, or wiki_delete_space

flags.name: task-id
flags.description: async task ID (for drive task_check, wiki_move, or wiki_delete_space tasks)

description: Upload a local file to Drive

flags.name: file
flags.description: local file path (files > 20MB use multipart upload automatically)

flags.name: file-token
flags.description: existing file token to overwrite in place

flags.name: folder-token
flags.description: target folder token (default: root folder; mutually exclusive with --wiki-token)

flags.name: name
flags.description: uploaded file name (default: local file name)

flags.name: wiki-token
flags.description: target wiki node token (uploads under that wiki node; mutually exclusive with --folder-token)

description: Delete a specific historical version of a Drive file

flags.name: version
flags.description: version from drive +version-history to delete (not tag)

description: Download a specific version of a Drive file

flags.name: output
flags.description: local save path or directory; omit to save into the current directory using the server filename

description: List the version history of a Drive file

flags.name: cursor
flags.description: pagination cursor from the previous page's next_cursor

description: Revert a Drive file to a specific historical version

flags.name: version
flags.description: version from drive +version-history to revert to (not tag)

description: Create a group chat or topic chat; user/bot; --chat-mode group|topic; private/public; invites users/bots; optionally sets bot manager

flags.name: bots
flags.description: comma-separated bot app IDs (cli_xxx) to invite, max 5

flags.name: chat-mode
flags.description: group mode ("topic" creates a topic chat; differs from a normal group in topic-message mode) (group|topic) (default "group")

flags.name: name
flags.description: group name (required for public groups, max 60 chars)

flags.name: owner
flags.description: owner open_id (ou_xxx); defaults to bot (--as bot) or authorized user (--as user)

flags.name: set-bot-manager
flags.description: the bot that creates this chat as manager (bot identity only)

flags.name: users
flags.description: comma-separated user open_ids (ou_xxx) to invite, max 50

description: List groups the current user/bot is a member of; user/bot; supports sorting, pagination, and --exclude-muted (user identity only)

flags.name: exclude-muted
flags.description: identity only) drop chats the current user has muted (do-not-disturb); bot identity returns all chats unfiltered

flags.name: user-id-type
flags.description: ID type for owner_id in response (open_id|union_id|user_id) (default "open_id")

description: List messages in a chat or P2P conversation; user/bot; accepts --chat-id or --user-id, resolves P2P chat_id, supports time range/sort/pagination

flags.name: user-id
flags.description: (required, mutually exclusive with --chat-id; user identity only) user open_id (ou_xxx)

description: Search visible group chats by --query keyword and/or --member-ids; user/bot; e.g. look up chat_id by group name; supports type filters, sorting, pagination, and --exclude-muted (user identity only)

flags.name: disable-search-by-user
flags.description: search-by-member-name (default: search by member name first, then group name)

flags.name: exclude-muted
flags.description: identity only) drop chats the current user has muted (do-not-disturb); bot identity returns all chats unfiltered

flags.name: is-manager
flags.description: show chats you created or manage

flags.name: search-types
flags.description: chat types, comma-separated (private, external, public_joined, public_not_joined)

description: Update group chat name or description; user/bot; updates a chat's name or description
Cancel (remove) a bookmark. When no --flag-type is given, performs double-cancel: removes both message and feed layers

flags.name: flag-type
flags.description: flag type override: message|feed; omit to double-cancel both layers

description: Create a bookmark on a message; user-only; defaults to message-layer flag; use --flag-type feed to create feed-layer flag (auto-detects chat type)
List bookmarks; user-only; auto-enriches feed-type thread entries with message content; supports `--page-all` auto-pagination

flags.name: enrich-feed-thread
flags.description: message content for feed-type thread entries (default true; may call messages/mget and require im:message.group_msg:get_as_user/im:message.p2p_msg:get_as_user; use --enrich-feed-thread=false to avoid extra scopes) (default true)

flags.name: page-limit
flags.description: max pages when auto-pagination is enabled (default 20, max 1000) (default 20)

description: Batch get messages by IDs; user/bot; fetches up to 50 om_ message IDs, formats sender names, expands thread replies
Reply to a message (supports thread replies); user/bot; supports text/markdown/post/media replies, reply-in-thread, idempotency key

flags.name: markdown
flags.description: markdown text (auto-wrapped as post format with style optimization; image URLs auto-resolved)

flags.name: msg-type
flags.description: message type for --content JSON; when using --text/--markdown/--image/--file/--video/--audio, the effective type is inferred automatically (text|post|image|file|audio|media|interactive|share_chat|share_user) (default "text")

flags.name: reply-in-thread
flags.description: in thread (message appears in thread stream instead of main chat)

flags.name: video
flags.description: video file_key, local file path; must be used together with --video-cover

flags.name: video-cover
flags.description: video cover image_key, local file path; required when using --video

description: Download images/files from a message; user/bot; downloads image/file resources by message-id and file-key to a safe relative output path

flags.name: output
flags.description: local save path (relative only, no .. traversal); when omitted, uses the server's Content-Disposition filename if available, otherwise file_key; extension is inferred from Content-Disposition or Content-Type if not provided

description: Search messages across chats (supports keyword, sender, time range filters) with user identity; user-only; filters by chat/sender/attachment/time, enriches results via mget and chats batch_query

flags.name: at-chatter-ids
flags.description: filter by @mentioned user open_ids, comma-separated (also matches messages that @all)

flags.name: end
flags.description: end time(ISO 8601) with local timezone offset (e.g. 2026-03-25T23:59:59+08:00)

flags.name: page-limit
flags.description: max search pages when auto-pagination is enabled (default 20, max 40) (default 20)

flags.name: start
flags.description: start time(ISO 8601) with local timezone offset (e.g. 2026-03-24T00:00:00+08:00)

description: Send a message to a chat or direct message; user/bot; sends to chat-id or user-id with text/markdown/post/media, supports idempotency key

flags.name: markdown
flags.description: markdown text (auto-wrapped as post format with style optimization; image URLs auto-resolved)

flags.name: msg-type
flags.description: message type for --content JSON; when using --text/--markdown/--image/--file/--video/--audio, the effective type is inferred automatically (text|post|image|file|audio|media|interactive|share_chat|share_user) (default "text")

flags.name: video
flags.description: video file_key, local file path; must be used together with --video-cover

flags.name: video-cover
flags.description: video cover image_key, local file path; required when using --video

description: List messages in a thread; user/bot; accepts om_/omt_ input, resolves message IDs to thread_id, supports sort/pagination

Dismiss the read-receipt request banner on an incoming mail by clearing its READ_RECEIPT_REQUEST label, without sending a receipt. Use when the user wants to silence the prompt but refuse to confirm they have read it. Idempotent — safe to re-run.

flags.name: mailbox
flags.description: Mailbox email address that owns the message (default: me).

flags.name: message-id
flags.description: Required. Message ID of the incoming mail that requested a read receipt.

description: Create a brand-new mail draft from scratch (NOT for reply or forward). For reply drafts use +reply; for forward drafts use +forward. Only use +draft-create when composing a new email with no parent message.

flags.name: attach
flags.description: Optional. Regular attachment file paths (relative path only). Separate multiple paths with commas. Each path must point to a readable local file.

flags.name: bcc
flags.description: Optional. Full Bcc recipient list. Separate multiple addresses with commas. Display-name format is supported.

flags.name: body
flags.description: Full email body. Prefer HTML for rich formatting (bold, lists, links); plain text is also supported. Body type is auto-detected. Use --plain-text to force plain-text mode. Required unless --template-id supplies a non-empty body.

flags.name: cc
flags.description: Optional. Full Cc recipient list. Separate multiple addresses with commas. Display-name format is supported.

flags.name: event-end
flags.description: Event end time (ISO 8601). Required when --event-summary is set.

flags.name: event-start
flags.description: Event start time (ISO 8601, e.g. 2026-04-20T14:00+08:00). Required when --event-summary is set.

flags.name: event-summary
flags.description: Calendar event title. Setting this enables calendar invitation mode.

flags.name: from
flags.description: Optional. Sender email address for the From header. When using an alias (send_as) address, set this to the alias and use --mailbox for the owning mailbox. If omitted, the mailbox's primary address is used.

flags.name: inline
flags.description: Optional. Inline images as a JSON array. Each entry: {"cid":"","file_path":""}. All file_path values must be relative paths. Cannot be used with --plain-text. CID images are embedded via in the HTML body. CID is a unique identifier, e.g. a random hex string like "a1b2c3d4e5f6a7b8c9d0".

flags.name: mailbox
flags.description: Optional. Mailbox email address that owns the draft (default: falls back to --from, then me). Use this when the sender (--from) differs from the mailbox, e.g. sending via an alias or send_as address.

flags.name: plain-text
flags.description: plain-text mode, ignoring HTML auto-detection. Cannot be used with --inline.

flags.name: priority
flags.description: Email priority: high, normal, low. If omitted, no priority header is set.

flags.name: request-receipt
flags.description: a read receipt (Message Disposition Notification, RFC 3798) addressed to the sender. Recipient mail clients may prompt the user, send automatically, or silently ignore — delivery of a receipt is not guaranteed.

flags.name: signature-id
flags.description: +signature   Optional. Signature ID to append after body content. Run mail +signature to list available signatures.

flags.name: subject
flags.description: Final draft subject. Pass the full subject you want to appear in the draft. Required unless --template-id supplies a non-empty subject.

flags.name: template-id
flags.description: Optional. Apply a saved template by ID (decimal integer string) before composing. The template's subject/body/to/cc/bcc/attachments are merged with user-supplied flags (user flags win). Requires --as user.

flags.name: to
flags.description: Optional. Full To recipient list. Separate multiple addresses with commas. Display-name format is supported. When omitted, the draft is created without recipients (they can be added later via +draft-edit).

description: Use when updating an existing mail draft without sending it. Prefer this shortcut over calling raw drafts.get or drafts.update directly, because it performs draft-safe MIME read/patch/write editing while preserving unchanged structure, attachments, and headers where possible.

flags.name: draft-id
flags.description: Target draft ID. Required for real edits. It can be omitted only when using the --print-patch-template flag by itself.

flags.name: from
flags.description: Mailbox email address containing the draft (default: me). Prefer --mailbox for clarity; --from is kept for backward compatibility. (default "me")

flags.name: inspect
flags.description: the draft without modifying it. Returns the draft projection including subject, recipients, body summary, has_quoted_content (whether the draft contains a reply/forward quote block), attachments_summary (with part_id and cid for each attachment), and inline_summary. Run this BEFORE editing body to check has_quoted_content: if true, use set_reply_body in --patch-file to preserve the quote; if false, use set_body.

flags.name: mailbox
flags.description: Mailbox email address that owns the draft (default: falls back to --from, then me). Takes priority over --from when both are set.

flags.name: patch-file
flags.description: Edit entry point for body edits, incremental recipient changes, header edits, attachment changes, or inline-image changes. All body edits MUST go through --patch-file. Two body ops: set_body (full replacement including quote) and set_reply_body (replaces only user-authored content, auto-preserves quote block). Run --inspect first to check has_quoted_content, then --print-patch-template for the JSON structure. Relative path only.

flags.name: print-patch-template
flags.description: the JSON template and supported operations for the --patch-file flag. Recommended first step before generating a patch file. No draft read or write is performed.

flags.name: remove-event
flags.description: the calendar event from the draft.

flags.name: request-receipt
flags.description: a read receipt (Message Disposition Notification, RFC 3798) addressed to the draft's sender. Recipient mail clients may prompt the user, send automatically, or silently ignore — delivery of a receipt is not guaranteed. Adds the Disposition-Notification-To header; existing value is overwritten.

flags.name: set-bcc
flags.description: Replace the entire Bcc recipient list with the addresses provided here. Separate multiple addresses with commas. Display-name format is supported.

flags.name: set-cc
flags.description: Replace the entire Cc recipient list with the addresses provided here. Separate multiple addresses with commas. Display-name format is supported.

flags.name: set-event-summary
flags.description: Set calendar event title. Must be used together with --set-event-start and --set-event-end.

flags.name: set-priority
flags.description: Set email priority: high, normal, low. Setting 'normal' removes any existing priority header.

flags.name: set-subject
flags.description: Replace the subject with this final value. Use this for full subject replacement, not for appending a fragment to the existing subject.

flags.name: set-to
flags.description: Replace the entire To recipient list with the addresses provided here. Separate multiple addresses with commas. Display-name format is supported.

description: Forward a message and save as draft (default). Use --confirm-send to send immediately after user confirmation. Original message block included automatically.

flags.name: attach
flags.description: Attachment file path(s), comma-separated, appended after original attachments (relative path only)

flags.name: body
flags.description: Body prepended before the forwarded message. Prefer HTML for rich formatting; plain text is also supported. Body type is auto-detected from the forward body and the original message. Use --plain-text to force plain-text mode.

flags.name: confirm-send
flags.description: the forward immediately instead of saving as draft. Only use after the user has explicitly confirmed recipients and content.

flags.name: event-end
flags.description: Event end time (ISO 8601). Required when --event-summary is set.

flags.name: event-start
flags.description: Event start time (ISO 8601, e.g. 2026-04-20T14:00+08:00). Required when --event-summary is set.

flags.name: event-summary
flags.description: Calendar event title. Setting this enables calendar invitation mode.

flags.name: from
flags.description: Sender email address for the From header. When using an alias (send_as) address, set this to the alias and use --mailbox for the owning mailbox. Defaults to the mailbox's primary address.

flags.name: inline
flags.description: Inline images as a JSON array. Each entry: {"cid":"","file_path":""}. All file_path values must be relative paths. Cannot be used with --plain-text. CID images are embedded via in the HTML body. CID is a unique identifier, e.g. a random hex string like "a1b2c3d4e5f6a7b8c9d0".

flags.name: mailbox
flags.description: Mailbox email address that owns the draft (default: falls back to --from, then me). Use this when the sender (--from) differs from the mailbox, e.g. sending via an alias or send_as address.

flags.name: plain-text
flags.description: plain-text mode, ignoring all HTML auto-detection. Cannot be used with --inline.

flags.name: priority
flags.description: Email priority: high, normal, low. If omitted, no priority header is set.

flags.name: request-receipt
flags.description: a read receipt (Message Disposition Notification, RFC 3798) addressed to the sender. Recipient mail clients may prompt the user, send automatically, or silently ignore — delivery of a receipt is not guaranteed.

flags.name: send-time
flags.description: Scheduled send time as a Unix timestamp in seconds. Must be at least 5 minutes in the future. Use with --confirm-send to schedule the email.

flags.name: signature-id
flags.description: +signature   Optional. Signature ID to append after body content. Run mail +signature to list available signatures.

flags.name: subject
flags.description: Optional. Override the auto-generated Fw: subject. When set, the shortcut uses this value verbatim instead of prefixing the original subject.

flags.name: template-id
flags.description: Optional. Apply a saved template by ID (decimal integer string) before composing. The template's body/to/cc/bcc/attachments are merged into the forward draft (template values appended to user flags / forward-derived values; no de-duplication).

description: Use when reading full content for a single email by message ID. Returns normalized body content plus attachments metadata, including inline images.

flags.name: html
flags.description: to return HTML body (false returns plain text only to save bandwidth) (default true)

flags.name: print-output-schema
flags.description: output field reference (run this first to learn field names before parsing output)

description: Use when reading full content for multiple emails by message ID. Prefer this shortcut over calling raw mail user_mailbox.messages batch_get directly, because it base64url-decodes body fields and returns normalized per-message output that is easier to consume.

flags.name: html
flags.description: to return HTML body (false returns plain text only to save bandwidth) (default true)

flags.name: message-ids
flags.description: Required. Comma-separated email message IDs. Example: "id1,id2,id3"

flags.name: print-output-schema
flags.description: output field reference (run this first to learn field names before parsing output)

description: Reply to a message and save as draft (default). Use --confirm-send to send immediately after user confirmation. Sets Re: subject, In-Reply-To, and References headers automatically.

flags.name: attach
flags.description: Attachment file path(s), comma-separated (relative path only)

flags.name: body
flags.description: Reply body. Prefer HTML for rich formatting; plain text is also supported. Body type is auto-detected from the reply body and the original message. Use --plain-text to force plain-text mode. Required unless --template-id supplies a non-empty body.

flags.name: confirm-send
flags.description: the reply immediately instead of saving as draft. Only use after the user has explicitly confirmed recipients and content.

flags.name: event-end
flags.description: Event end time (ISO 8601). Required when --event-summary is set.

flags.name: event-start
flags.description: Event start time (ISO 8601, e.g. 2026-04-20T14:00+08:00). Required when --event-summary is set.

flags.name: event-summary
flags.description: Calendar event title. Setting this enables calendar invitation mode.

flags.name: from
flags.description: Sender email address for the From header. When using an alias (send_as) address, set this to the alias and use --mailbox for the owning mailbox. Defaults to the mailbox's primary address.

flags.name: inline
flags.description: Inline images as a JSON array. Each entry: {"cid":"","file_path":""}. All file_path values must be relative paths. Cannot be used with --plain-text. CID images are embedded via in the HTML body. CID is a unique identifier, e.g. a random hex string like "a1b2c3d4e5f6a7b8c9d0".

flags.name: mailbox
flags.description: Mailbox email address that owns the draft (default: falls back to --from, then me). Use this when the sender (--from) differs from the mailbox, e.g. sending via an alias or send_as address.

flags.name: message-id
flags.description: Required. Message ID to reply to

flags.name: plain-text
flags.description: plain-text mode, ignoring all HTML auto-detection. Cannot be used with --inline.

flags.name: priority
flags.description: Email priority: high, normal, low. If omitted, no priority header is set.

flags.name: request-receipt
flags.description: a read receipt (Message Disposition Notification, RFC 3798) addressed to the sender. Recipient mail clients may prompt the user, send automatically, or silently ignore — delivery of a receipt is not guaranteed.

flags.name: send-time
flags.description: Scheduled send time as a Unix timestamp in seconds. Must be at least 5 minutes in the future. Use with --confirm-send to schedule the email.

flags.name: signature-id
flags.description: +signature   Optional. Signature ID to append after body content. Run mail +signature to list available signatures.

flags.name: subject
flags.description: Optional. Override the auto-generated Re: subject. When set, the shortcut uses this value verbatim instead of prefixing the original subject.

flags.name: template-id
flags.description: Optional. Apply a saved template by ID (decimal integer string) before composing. The template's body/to/cc/bcc/attachments are appended to the reply-derived values (no de-duplication; see warning in Execute output).

flags.name: to
flags.description: Additional To address(es), comma-separated (appended to original sender's address)

flags.name: attach

flags.name: confirm-send

flags.name: inline
flags.description: Inline images as a JSON array. Each entry: {"cid":"","file_path":""}. All file_path values must be relative paths. Cannot be used with --plain-text. CID images are embedded via in the HTML body. CID is a unique identifier, e.g. a random hex string like "a1b2c3d4e5f6a7b8c9d0".

flags.name: mailbox
flags.description: Mailbox email address that owns the draft (default: falls back to --from, then me). Use this when the sender (--from) differs from the mailbox, e.g. sending via an alias or send_as address.

flags.name: message-id

flags.name: priority
flags.description: Email priority: high, normal, low. If omitted, no priority header is set.

flags.name: remove

flags.name: request-receipt

flags.name: signature-id

flags.name: subject

description: Compose a new email and save as draft (default). Use --confirm-send to send immediately after user confirmation.

flags.name: attach
flags.description: Attachment file path(s), comma-separated (relative path only)

flags.name: body
flags.description: Email body. Prefer HTML for rich formatting (bold, lists, links); plain text is also supported. Body type is auto-detected. Use --plain-text to force plain-text mode. Required unless --template-id supplies a non-empty body.

flags.name: confirm-send
flags.description: the email immediately instead of saving as draft. Only use after the user has explicitly confirmed recipients and content.

flags.name: event-end
flags.description: Event end time (ISO 8601). Required when --event-summary is set.

flags.name: event-start
flags.description: Event start time (ISO 8601, e.g. 2026-04-20T14:00+08:00). Required when --event-summary is set.

flags.name: event-summary
flags.description: Calendar event title. Setting this enables calendar invitation mode.

flags.name: from
flags.description: Sender email address for the From header. When using an alias (send_as) address, set this to the alias and use --mailbox for the owning mailbox. Defaults to the mailbox's primary address.

flags.name: inline
flags.description: Inline images as a JSON array. Each entry: {"cid":"","file_path":""}. All file_path values must be relative paths. Cannot be used with --plain-text. CID images are embedded via in the HTML body. CID is a unique identifier, e.g. a random hex string like "a1b2c3d4e5f6a7b8c9d0".

flags.name: mailbox
flags.description: Mailbox email address that owns the draft (default: falls back to --from, then me). Use this when the sender (--from) differs from the mailbox, e.g. sending via an alias or send_as address.

flags.name: plain-text
flags.description: plain-text mode, ignoring HTML auto-detection. Cannot be used with --inline.

flags.name: priority
flags.description: Email priority: high, normal, low. If omitted, no priority header is set.

flags.name: request-receipt
flags.description: a read receipt (Message Disposition Notification, RFC 3798) addressed to the sender. Recipient mail clients may prompt the user, send automatically, or silently ignore — delivery of a receipt is not guaranteed.

flags.name: send-time
flags.description: Scheduled send time as a Unix timestamp in seconds. Must be at least 5 minutes in the future. Use with --confirm-send to schedule the email.

flags.name: signature-id
flags.description: +signature   Optional. Signature ID to append after body content. Run mail +signature to list available signatures.

flags.name: subject
flags.description: Email subject. Required unless --template-id supplies a non-empty subject.

flags.name: template-id
flags.description: Optional. Apply a saved template by ID (decimal integer string) before composing. The template's subject/body/to/cc/bcc/attachments are merged with user-supplied flags (user flags win). Requires --as user.

description: Send a read-receipt reply for an incoming message that requested one (i.e. carries the READ_RECEIPT_REQUEST label). Body is auto-generated (subject / recipient / send time / read time) to match the Lark client's receipt format — callers cannot customize it, matching the industry norm that read-receipt bodies are system-generated templates, not free-form replies. Intended for agent use after the user confirms.

flags.name: from
flags.description: Sender email address for the From header. Defaults to the mailbox's primary address.

flags.name: mailbox
flags.description: Mailbox email address that owns the receipt reply (default: me).

flags.name: message-id
flags.description: Required. Message ID of the incoming mail that requested a read receipt.

description: Share an email or thread as a card to a Lark IM chat.

flags.name: message-id
flags.description: Message ID to share (mutually exclusive with --thread-id)

flags.name: receive-id-type
flags.description: Receiver ID type: chat_id (default), open_id, user_id, union_id, email (default "chat_id")

flags.name: thread-id
flags.description: Thread ID to share (mutually exclusive with --message-id)

description: List or view email signatures with default usage info.

flags.name: detail
flags.description: Signature ID to view rendered details. Omit to list all signatures.

description: Create a personal mail template. Scans HTML local paths (reusing draft inline-image detection), uploads inline images and non-inline attachments to Drive, rewrites HTML to cid: references, and POSTs a Template payload to mail.user_mailbox.templates.create.

flags.name: attach
flags.description: Optional. Non-inline attachment file path(s), comma-separated (relative path only). Each file is uploaded to Drive; the order follows the flag order exactly (order-sensitive for LARGE/SMALL classification).

flags.name: bcc
flags.description: Optional. Default Bcc recipient list. Separate multiple addresses with commas.

flags.name: cc
flags.description: Optional. Default Cc recipient list. Separate multiple addresses with commas.

flags.name: mailbox
flags.description: Mailbox email address that owns the template (default: me).

flags.name: plain-text
flags.description: the template as plain-text mode (is_plain_text_mode=true). Inline images still require HTML content; use only for pure plain-text templates.

flags.name: subject
flags.description: Optional. Default subject saved with the template.

flags.name: template-content
flags.description: Template body content. Prefer HTML. Referenced local images () are auto-uploaded to Drive and rewritten to cid: refs.

flags.name: template-content-file
flags.description: Optional. Path to a file whose contents become --template-content. Relative path only. Mutually exclusive with --template-content.

flags.name: to
flags.description: Optional. Default To recipient list. Separate multiple addresses with commas. Display-name format is supported.

description: Update an existing mail template. Supports --inspect (read-only projection), --print-patch-template (prints a JSON skeleton for --patch-file), and flat flags (--set-subject / --set-name / etc). Internally it GETs the template, applies the patch, rewrites local paths to cid: refs, and PUTs a full-replace update (no optimistic locking: last-write-wins).

flags.name: attach
flags.description: Additional non-inline attachment file path(s), comma-separated. Each file is uploaded to Drive and appended to the template's attachments[] in the exact flag order.

flags.name: inspect
flags.description: the template without modifying it. Returns the current template projection (name/subject/addresses/attachments). No write is performed.

flags.name: mailbox
flags.description: Mailbox email address that owns the template (default: me).

flags.name: patch-file
flags.description: Path to a JSON patch file (relative path only). Shape is the same as --print-patch-template output.

flags.name: print-patch-template
flags.description: a JSON template describing the supported --patch-file structure. No network call is made.

flags.name: set-bcc
flags.description: Replace the Bcc recipient list. Pass --set-bcc="" to clear the list.

flags.name: set-cc
flags.description: Replace the Cc recipient list. Pass --set-cc="" to clear the list.

flags.name: set-template-content
flags.description: Replace the template body content. Prefer HTML for rich formatting.

flags.name: set-template-content-file
flags.description: Replace template body content with the contents of a file (relative path only). Mutually exclusive with --set-template-content.

flags.name: set-to
flags.description: Replace the To recipient list. Separate multiple addresses with commas. Pass --set-to="" to clear the list.

flags.name: template-id
flags.description: Template ID to update. Required except when using --print-patch-template by itself.

description: Use when querying a full mail conversation/thread by thread ID. Returns all messages in chronological order, including replies and drafts, with body content and attachments metadata, including inline images.

flags.name: html
flags.description: to return HTML body (false returns plain text only to save bandwidth) (default true)

flags.name: include-spam-trash
flags.description: return messages from SPAM and TRASH folders (excluded by default)

flags.name: print-output-schema
flags.description: output field reference (run this first to learn field names before parsing output)

description: List mail summaries (date/from/subject/message_id). Use --query for full-text search, --filter for exact-match conditions.

flags.name: filter
flags.description: exact-match condition filter (JSON). Narrow results by folder, label, sender, recipient, etc. Run --print-filter-schema to see all fields. Example: {"folder":"INBOX","from":["alice@example.com"]}

flags.name: max
flags.description: maximum number of messages to fetch (1-400; auto-paginates internally) (default 20)

flags.name: page-token
flags.description: pagination token from a previous response to fetch the next page

flags.name: query
flags.description: full-text keyword search across from/to/subject/body (max 50 chars). Example: "budget report"

description: Watch for incoming mail events via WebSocket (requires scope mail:event and bot event mail.user_mailbox.event.message_received_v1 added). Run with --print-output-schema to see per-format field reference before parsing output.

flags.name: folder-ids
flags.description: filter: folder IDs JSON array, e.g. ["INBOX","SENT"]

flags.name: folders
flags.description: filter: folder names JSON array, e.g. ["inbox","news"]

flags.name: label-ids
flags.description: filter: label IDs JSON array, e.g. ["FLAGGED","IMPORTANT"]

flags.name: labels
flags.description: filter: label names JSON array, e.g. ["important","team-label"]

flags.name: msg-format
flags.description: message payload mode: metadata(headers + meta, for triage/notification) | minimal(IDs and state only, no headers, for tracking read/folder changes) | plain_text_full(all metadata fields + full plain-text body) | event(raw WebSocket event, no API call, for debug) | full(full message including HTML body and attachments) (default "metadata")

flags.name: output-dir
flags.description: Write each message as a JSON file (always full payload, regardless of --msg-format)

flags.name: print-output-schema
flags.description: output field reference per --msg-format (run this first to learn field names before parsing output)

description: Create a Markdown file in Drive

flags.name: folder-token
flags.description: target Drive folder token (default: root folder)

flags.name: name
flags.description: file name with .md suffix; required with --content, optional with --file

description: Fetch a Markdown file from Drive

flags.name: output
flags.description: local save path or directory; omit to return content directly

description: Overwrite an existing Markdown file in Drive

flags.name: name
flags.description: optional file name with .md suffix; overrides the existing/local file name

description: Patch a Markdown file in Drive via fetch-local-replace-overwrite

flags.name: pattern
flags.description: literal text or RE2 regex to match (supports @file, - for stdin)

description: Download audio/video media file of a minute

flags.name: minute-tokens
flags.description: minute tokens, comma-separated for batch download (max 50)

flags.name: url-only
flags.description: print the download URL(s) without downloading

description: Search minutes by keyword, owners, participants, and time range

flags.name: owner-ids
flags.description: owner open_id list, comma-separated (use "me" for current user)

flags.name: participant-ids
flags.description: participant open_id list, comma-separated (use "me" for current user)

description: Upload a media file token to generate a minute

flags.name: file-token
flags.description: file_token of a supported audio/video file already uploaded to Drive

description: List objectives and key results under an OKR cycle
List okr cycles of a certain user

flags.name: time-range
flags.description: specify time range. Use Format as YYYY-MM--YYYY-MM. leave empty to fetch all user cycles.

flags.name: user-id-type
flags.description: user ID type: open_id | union_id | user_id (default "open_id")

description: Create an OKR progress

flags.name: content
flags.description: progress content in ContentBlock JSON format (supports @file, - for stdin)

flags.name: progress-status
flags.description: progress status: normal | overdue | done. must provided with --progress-percent (normal|overdue|done)

flags.name: source-title
flags.description: source title for display (default "created by lark-cli")

flags.name: source-url
flags.description: source URL for display (defaults to open platform URL based on brand)

flags.name: target-id
flags.description: target ID (objective or key result ID)

flags.name: user-id-type
flags.description: user ID type: open_id | union_id | user_id (default "open_id")

description: Update sheet properties

flags.description: freeze columns through this count (0 unfreezes)

flags.description: freeze rows through this count (0 unfreezes)

flags.name: image
flags.description: local image file path (supported formats: PNG, JPEG, JPG, GIF, BMP, JFIF, EXIF, TIFF, BPG, HEIC)

flags.name: name
flags.description: image file name with extension (defaults to the basename of --image)

flags.description: target cell (e.g. A1 or <sheetId>!A1). Start and end cell must be the same

description: Create a Lark Slides presentation

flags.name: slides
flags.description: slide content JSON array (each element is a XML string, max 10; for more pages, create first then add via xml_presentation.slide.create). placeholders are auto-uploaded and replaced with file_token.

description: Upload a local image to a slides presentation and return the file_token (use as )

flags.name: presentation
flags.description: xml_presentation_id, slides URL, or wiki URL that resolves to slides

description: Replace elements on a slide via block_replace / block_insert parts (auto-injects id + <content/> on shape elements)

flags.name: parts
flags.description: JSON array of replace parts (each: {action: block_replace|block_insert, ...}); max 200 (supports @file, - for stdin)

flags.name: presentation
flags.description: xml_presentation_id, slides URL, or wiki URL that resolves to slides

flags.name: revision-id
flags.description: presentation revision (-1 = latest; pass a specific number for optimistic locking) (default -1)

flags.name: tid
flags.description: transaction id for concurrent-edit locking (usually empty)

description: assign or remove task members

flags.name: add
flags.description: comma-separated assignee IDs to add; use open_id (ou_xxx) when assignee is user, use app id (cli_xxx) when assignee is app

flags.name: idempotency-key
flags.description: client token for idempotency (used for add_members)

flags.name: remove
flags.description: comma-separated assignee IDs to remove; use open_id (ou_xxx) when assignee is user, use app id (cli_xxx) when assignee is app

description: add a comment to a task
mark a task as complete
create a task

flags.name: assignee
flags.description: task assignee id added during create; use open_id (ou_xxx) when assignee is user, use app id (cli_xxx) when assignee is app

flags.name: due
flags.description: due date (ISO 8601 / date:YYYY-MM-DD / relative:+2d / ms timestamp)

flags.name: follower
flags.description: task follower id added during create; use open_id (ou_xxx) when follower is user, use app id (cli_xxx) when follower is app

description: manage task followers

flags.name: add
flags.description: comma-separated follower IDs to add; use open_id (ou_xxx) when follower is user, use app id (cli_xxx) when follower is app

flags.name: idempotency-key
flags.description: client token for idempotency (used for add_members)

flags.name: remove
flags.description: comma-separated follower IDs to remove; use open_id (ou_xxx) when follower is user, use app id (cli_xxx) when follower is app

description: List tasks assigned to me

flags.name: complete
flags.description: true, query completed tasks;if false, query incompleted tasks; if not provided, both completed and incompleted tasks are queried.

flags.name: created_at
flags.description: query tasks created after this time (date/relative/ms)

flags.name: due-end
flags.description: query tasks with due date before this time (date/relative/ms)

flags.name: due-start
flags.description: query tasks with due date after this time (date/relative/ms)

flags.name: page-token
flags.description: start from the specified page token

flags.name: query
flags.description: search for tasks by summary (exact match first, then partial match)

description: list tasks related to me

flags.name: created-by-me
flags.description: filter to tasks created by me; pagination still follows upstream related-task pages

flags.name: followed-by-me
flags.description: filter to tasks followed by me; pagination still follows upstream related-task pages

flags.name: include-complete
flags.description: true; set false to return only incomplete tasks

flags.name: page-token
flags.description: page token / updated_at cursor in microseconds

description: manage task reminders
reopen a completed task
search tasks

flags.name: completed
flags.description: true for completed or false for incomplete tasks

description: set or clear a task ancestor

flags.name: ancestor-id
flags.description: ancestor task guid; omit to make it independent

description: subscribe to task events
create a tasklist and optionally add tasks

flags.name: data
flags.description: JSON array of tasks to create within this tasklist

flags.name: member
flags.description: comma-separated open_ids to add as editors

description: manage tasklist members

flags.name: add
flags.description: comma-separated open_ids to add as members

flags.name: remove
flags.description: comma-separated open_ids to remove from members

flags.name: set
flags.description: comma-separated open_ids to set as exact members (replaces existing)

description: search tasklists
add tasks to a tasklist
update task attributes

flags.name: due
flags.description: due date (ISO 8601 / date:YYYY-MM-DD / relative:+2d / ms timestamp)

description: upload a local file as an attachment to a task; use --resource-type=task_delivery when uploading to task agents

flags.name: resource-type
flags.description: owning resource type (default: task); use task_delivery when uploading to task agents (default "task")

description: List bot meeting events by meeting ID

flags.name: end
flags.description: time upper bound (ISO 8601, YYYY-MM-DD, or Unix seconds)

flags.name: page-token
flags.description: page token for the next page

flags.name: start
flags.description: time lower bound (ISO 8601, YYYY-MM-DD, or Unix seconds)

description: Join a meeting by meeting number (bot join)
Leave a meeting by meeting ID
Query meeting notes (via meeting-ids, minute-tokens, or calendar-event-ids)

flags.name: calendar-event-ids
flags.description: calendar event instance IDs, comma-separated for batch

description: Query minute_token from meeting-ids or calendar-event-ids

flags.name: calendar-event-ids
flags.description: calendar event instance IDs, comma-separated for batch

description: Search meeting records (requires at least one filter)
Query a existing whiteboard, export it as preview image or raw nodes structure.

flags.name: output
flags.description: output directory. It is required when output as image. If not specified when --output_as code/raw, it will output directly.

flags.name: output_as
flags.description: output whiteboard as: image | code | raw.

flags.name: whiteboard-token
flags.description: whiteboard token of the whiteboard. You will need read permission to download preview image.

description: Update an existing whiteboard in lark document with mermaid, plantuml or whiteboard dsl. refer to lark-whiteboard skill for more details.

flags.name: idempotent-token
flags.description: idempotent token to ensure the update is idempotent. Default is empty. min length is 10.

flags.name: input_format
flags.description: format of input data: raw | plantuml | mermaid. Default is raw.

flags.name: overwrite
flags.description: the whiteboard content, delete all existing content before update. Default is false.

flags.name: whiteboard-token
flags.description: whiteboard token of the whiteboard to update. You will need edit permission to update the whiteboard.

description: Delete a wiki space, polling the async delete task when needed
Move a wiki node, or move a Drive document into Wiki

flags.name: apply
flags.description: a move request when the caller lacks permission to move the document immediately

flags.name: node-token
flags.description: wiki node token to move inside Wiki

flags.name: obj-token
flags.description: Drive document token for docs-to-wiki mode

flags.name: obj-type
flags.description: Drive document type for docs-to-wiki mode (doc|sheet|bitable|mindnote|docx|file|slides)

flags.name: source-space-id
flags.description: source wiki space ID for --node-token; if omitted, it is resolved from the node token

flags.name: target-parent-token
flags.description: target parent wiki node token; if omitted for docs-to-wiki, the document is moved to the target space root

flags.name: target-space-id
flags.description: target wiki space ID; required for docs-to-wiki, optional for node move when --target-parent-token is set

description: Copy a wiki node to a target space or parent node

flags.name: target-parent-node-token
flags.description: target parent node token; required if --target-space-id is not set

flags.name: target-space-id
flags.description: target wiki space ID; required if --target-parent-node-token is not set

flags.name: title
flags.description: new title for the copied node; leave empty to keep the original title

description: Create a wiki node with automatic space resolution

flags.name: parent-node-token
flags.description: parent wiki node token; if set, the new node is created under that parent

flags.name: space-id
flags.description: target wiki space ID; use my_library for the personal document library

description: List wiki nodes in a space or under a parent node

flags.name: page-limit
flags.description: max pages to fetch with --page-all (default 10, 0 = unlimited) (default 10)

flags.name: page-token
flags.description: page token; implies single-page fetch (no auto-pagination)

flags.name: parent-node-token
flags.description: parent node token; if omitted, lists the root-level nodes of the space

flags.name: space-id
flags.description: wiki space ID; use my_library for the personal document library, or +space-list to discover other space IDs

description: List wiki spaces accessible to the caller

flags.name: page-limit
flags.description: max pages to fetch with --page-all (default 10, 0 = unlimited) (default 10)

flags.name: page-token
flags.description: page token; implies single-page fetch (no auto-pagination)
